description.en: 'User Experience Design for a Physical Therapy Application' - that was the title of  my masters thesis. During my thesis I delved deep into the uses of modern technology for physiotherapy through the lens of an UX deisgner.

content.en: Following extensive research and iterative prototyping, I developed a comprehensive ecosystem designed to enhance physical rehabilitation. The system bridges the gap between patients and therapists: patients use a mobile app that utilizes AI body tracking to provide real-time feedback on exercises, while therapists monitor progress and adjust treatments via a dedicated web dashboard. To ensure the solution met real-world needs, I fully immersed myself in the user experience. Following a Design Thinking methodology, I utilized expert interviews, empathy maps, personas, and interaction storyboards to guide the development. This iterative process allowed me to transform complex clinical requirements into a seamless, user-centered exchange between patient and practitioner.

I started off creating empathy maps and personas to better empathize with the users and get a feel for their needs and pain points. Additonally, at this stage I also conducted expert interviews with physiotherapists and HCI experts
Based on this inital research I sketched interaction storyboards and created a user flow to outline the core functionality of the system and how patients and physiotherapists would interact with it.

The UI designs for both the desktop and mobile applications were created in Figma, with a strong focus on maintaining a modern and cohesive visual identity. The desktop version is primarily used by physiotherapists to monitor patient progress and adjust treatments.
bla bla add something about the mobiel version here

The technical prototype was developed primarily to demonstrate feasibility. By integrating Google MediaPipe, I was able to use a standard smartphone camera to track various exercises and calculate the quality of a user's movements in real-time. To provide this instant feedback, I developed the application as a web app using Visual Studio Code.
content.de: The technical prototype was developed primarily to demonstrate feasibility. By integrating Google MediaPipe, I was able to use a standard smartphone camera to track various exercises and calculate the quality of a user's movements in real-time. To provide this instant feedback, I developed the application as a web app using Visual Studio Code.

content.en: Reflecting on this journey, I found immense value in deep-diving into a complex, real-world challenge. Engaging directly with experts and users not only refined the project’s direction but also reinforced my passion for research-driven design. This experience taught me how to translate specialized professional needs into intuitive digital tools, and I truly enjoyed the process of turning expert insights into a meaningful, technical solution.
